beats.reasoning: A low-energy start fits yesterday’s 25/100, and the drizzle prompts a stay-in routine.

beats.activity: Grabs a quick breakfast-to-go and coffee from a nearby grocery to avoid morning rain errands.
beats.reasoning: Rain from early morning makes a quick, nearby grab feel efficient and realistic.

beats.activity: Drives toward Overlake for classes and lab time, building extra buffer for traffic.
beats.reasoning: The SR-520 eastbound closure 9am–noon means she leaves early to dodge the worst snarls.

beats.activity: Attends seminars and works through debugging in the computer lab while it’s steady rain outside.
beats.reasoning: Drizzly weather keeps her focused indoors, and leaving early avoids the bridge closure impact.

beats.activity: Has a warm lunch with classmates indoors and compares notes on upcoming assignments.
beats.reasoning: Rain makes an indoor-friendly lunch feel plausible, and she likes social time with her cohort.

beats.reasoning: Grey weather and her perfectionist streak fit a quick errand to get the right tools for planned work.

beats.activity: Heads back across the lake after the closure window ends, choosing the least stressful route.
beats.reasoning: The 9am–noon SR-520 closure eases after noon, so she can return without excessive risk.

beats.activity: Winds down at home, reorganizing her board-game bag and reviewing a camping checklist for the weekend.
beats.reasoning: With rain continuing until 2pm, she keeps her afternoon cozy and prepared.

beats.activity: Heads out to meet friends for board-game night planning, keeping the outing low-key and nearby.; Plays a couple quick rounds and chats between turns, then helps set up the table for later players.

beats.activity: At home, she showers, organizes notes for tomorrow, and does a low-effort leisure stretch with a light episode.

beats.activity: Falls asleep and lets the rain and busy day fade out.

diary:
Woke up to that familiar grey drizzle, the kind that makes the whole city look softer around the edges. From Renton I did my usual quick tidy and checked the rain plan like it was a weather forecast and a personal challenge. The morning coffee helped, but honestly the real lifesaver was ducking into Safeway on Capitol Hill for breakfast-to-go—no sense wrestling an umbrella before lab work.

Overlake in the steady rain felt focused, like the outside world couldn’t quite reach me. Around lunch at Bellevue Downtown Park we stayed indoors, trading notes on assignments and pretending the day’s stress was just “data,” not traffic. I restocked cables and notebooks at REI, then after the SR-520 closure window ended I crossed back with a sigh of relief. Board-game prep at Meeples & Dice in Bellevue was a bright little pocket. Even Pike Place Market at dusk felt cozy. Now I’m showered, notes organized, rain settling into silence, and I feel oddly calm—like the day behaved, eventually.
thought_process: I planned my morning around two forces: the rain and the SR-520 closure. That bridge problem would’ve turned a normal commute into a guessing game, so I built buffer early and chose errands that could be done under cover. Breakfast at Safeway was partly efficiency, partly mood—standing under storefront awnings feels less draining than calculating “just one more stop” in wet traffic. In the lab I stayed with debugging because my focus held better when the weather kept everything else muted. During lunch I intentionally compared notes with classmates to convert uncertainty into shared next steps. The world events definitely nudged my pacing; I felt sharper in the morning, then lighter after the cross-lake ride and the casual board-game planning at Meeples & Dice.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
